paper_title: The effect of an experimental decrease in salinity on the viability of the Subarctic planktonic foraminifera Neogloboquadrina incompta

methods.description: Process for collecting and incubating specimens of N. incompta to study their salinity tolerance under controlled conditions.

steps.description: Specimens of N. incompta were collected during a cruise on the RV Helmer Hansen in October 2018 to the shelf and slope of northern Norway off Tromsø.
steps.searchable_summary: collect N. incompta specimens from northern Norway shelf and slope

steps.description: Specimens were sampled from a water depth between 0 and 100 m, using a WP2 plankton net (63 µm mesh size) that was towed vertically.
steps.searchable_summary: use WP2 plankton net to collect specimens from 0-100 m depth

steps.description: The retrieved specimens were picked on board and incubated in jars containing seawater at 35 PSU previously filtered through a 0.22 µm nitrate cellulose filter and allowed to recover for ca. 16 hr at treatment temperature (6°C).
steps.searchable_summary: incubate specimens in filtered seawater at 35 PSU and 6°C for 16 hours
